articles.headline: How Are the Rules for Infant Formula Provision to Babies in Indonesia?
articles.summary: The IDAI criticized the BGN guidelines that allow mass distribution of infant formula in the MBG program, fearing it will reduce exclusive breastfeeding. The Kemenkes affirmed that infant formula may be given only when there is a clear medical indication and after counseling, in line with PP No 28/2024 and Permenkes 39/2013.
articles.keywords: infant formula,MBG program,IDAI,National Nutrition Agency,health regulations

articles.headline: BPOM Finds Dangerous Herbal Medicines Including Male Enhancement Drugs, Risking Cancer and Sudden Death
articles.summary: BPOM identified 22 natural‑product medicine brands that contain pharmaceutical chemicals, such as sildenafil, tadalafil, and methyl testosterone; 10 products have a NIE, while the other 12 are unregistered or fictitious. Most are male stamina supplements, posing risks of heart problems, stroke, and even death when used without medical supervision.
articles.keywords: BPOM, herbal medicine, chemicals, male stamina, health risk

articles.headline: BPJS Kesehatan Contributions Set to Rise, Check Rates as of 24 May 2026
articles.summary: Health Minister Budi Gunadi Sadikin said the 2026 BPJS Kesehatan contribution increase is due to a projected JKN deficit of Rp20‑30 trillion, with a new rate of about Rp42,000 per month for upper‑middle‑class members. The hike does not affect PBI participants and will only be applied if economic growth exceeds 6 %.
articles.keywords: BPJS Kesehatan, contribution increase, JKN deficit, Budi Gunadi Sadikin, 6% economy

articles.headline: DPR member urges the public to be smarter when choosing cosmetics and medicines
List of 22 Herbal Medicines Containing Hazardous Chemicals, BPOM: May Cause Heart Disorders
articles.summary: BPOM identified 22 herbal medicine products containing hazardous chemicals, including 13 male‑stamina products with sildenafil, tadalafil and methyl testosterone; 10 have a Distribution Permit number, while the other 12 lack one or use fictitious permits. BPOM head Taruna Ikrar warned that without a permit the products have not undergone safety evaluation and may cause heart problems, stroke or death.

articles.headline: BPOM Finds 22 Dangerous Herbal Medicines, Trigger Stroke and Kidney Failure
articles.summary: BPOM in Jakarta identified 22 brands of dangerous herbal medicines containing harsh chemicals; 10 listed fictitious distribution permits and 12 had no permit. The finding, based on intensive monitoring in March 2026, was presented by BPOM head Taruna Ikrar.

articles.headline: BPOM Uncovers 22 Herbal Medicines Containing Hazardous Chemicals, Potentially Causing Stroke to Kidney Failure
articles.summary: BPOM disclosed 22 herbal medicine brands that contain hard‑drug chemicals, including 13 men’s stamina products with sildenafil, tadalafil, paracetamol and methyl testosterone. Ten products have a Marketing Authorization Number, while twelve use fictitious permits. BPOM head Taruna Ikrar stressed that unlicensed products have not undergone safety evaluation.

articles.headline: List of 22 Dangerous Herbal Medicines Damaging Kidneys Widely Distributed, BPOM Findings Include Stamina Boosters
articles.summary: BPOM disclosed 22 hazardous herbal medicines on the market; 10 have a Distribution Permit Number while 12 lack one or are fictitious. Thirteen male‑stamina products contain hard‑drug chemicals such as sildenafil, tadalafil and methyl testosterone, which can damage kidneys, based on March 2026 surveillance.

articles.headline: These 22 natural‑product drug brands that BPOM says contain chemicals
articles.summary: BPOM disclosed 22 natural‑product drug brands containing chemicals such as sildenafil, tadalafil and paracetamol in its March 2026 surveillance. BPOM head Taruna Ikrar in Jakarta said 10 products have marketing authorization, while the other 12 lack it or have fictitious NIEs, posing serious health risks.
articles.keywords: BPOM, natural‑product drug, chemical, sildenafil, Jakarta

articles.headline: BPOM investigates 22 dangerous natural‑ingredient medicines, most of them male stamina products
articles.summary: Jakarta (ANTARA) – BPOM identified 22 brands of Obat Bahan Alam (OBA) that contained Bahan Kimia Obat (BKO) during the March 2026 monitoring period. Thirteen were male stamina products, and BPOM is conducting further investigations.

articles.headline: BBPOM Makassar uncovers illegal mercury-based cosmetics production
articles.summary: On 19 May 2026, BBPOM Makassar uncovered an illegal cosmetics factory in Makassar City, seizing evidence worth Rp700 million that contained mercury and hydroquinone. The finding was announced at a press conference on 21 September, highlighting consumer health risks.
articles.keywords: BBPOM, Makassar, illegal cosmetics, mercury, hydroquinone

articles.headline: BPOM data: 14 million UMKM not registered, aims to help them upgrade
articles.summary: BPOM revealed that only 4.2 million UMKM are registered out of 14 million unrecorded, and will launch the SAPA UMKM program for data collection and licensing assistance. BPOM head Taruna Ikrar said the goal is to help UMKM move up the value chain through 83 UPT units across Indonesia.
articles.keywords: BPOM, UMKM, SAPA UMKM, Taruna Ikrar, registration

articles.headline: Emergency Illegal Medicine Distribution in Bali, BPOM Seizes 173,000 Tablets, 15 People Charged
articles.summary: BPOM and the police in Denpasar seized 173,000 illegal medicine tablets worth Rp200 million and charged 15 suspects in the 2023‑2026 operation. Deputy Enforcement Tubagus Ade Hidayt emphasized efforts to break the OOT distribution chain that threatens the mental health of Bali’s population.
articles.keywords: BPOM, illegal medicine distribution, Denpasar, Tubagus Ade Hidayt, 173,000 tablets

articles.headline: Deputy Health Minister stresses nicotine addiction threatens Indonesia's Golden Vision
articles.summary: Deputy Health Minister Benjamin Paulus Octavianus said nicotine addiction threatens Indonesia’s Golden Vision while speaking at the Indonesia Conference on Tobacco Control 2026 at Universitas Airlangga in Surabaya. He emphasized the need for strict vape regulations and a fair transition policy for tobacco farmers.
articles.keywords: Benjamin Paulus, nicotine addiction, ICTOH 2026, vape regulation, Surabaya

articles.headline: BPOM Tarakan Holds Public Consultation Forum in Tanjung Selor, Discusses Service Standards and Drug Education
articles.summary: BPOM Tarakan held the 2026 Public Consultation Forum in the Bulungan Regency Inspectorate Office, North Kalimantan, on 21 May. Head Iswadi presented draft service standards and drug‑food safety education, opening dialogue with the public and stakeholders to boost participation and improve BPOM oversight.

articles.headline: DPR pushes national mitigation to anticipate entry of Andes hantavirus
articles.summary: Komisi IX DPR RI member Ravindra Airlangga emphasized the need for quarantine‑facility readiness at airports and seaports, and comprehensive screening of passengers from high‑risk countries, to build an integrated national mitigation system for detecting and handling Andes hantavirus.

articles.headline: BPOM Highlights SOP and Distribution of SPPG Kaliwates 3 Food Causing Poisoning of 22 Kindergarten Students
articles.summary: BPOM Jember examined the processing and distribution procedures of SPPG Kaliwates 3 food after 22 kindergarten children were suspected of MBG poisoning. Inspector Yusita Harminingsih stressed the need for strict SOPs and 2×24‑hour sample storage for traceability. The cause is still under investigation.
articles.keywords: BPOM, SPPG Kaliwates, poisoning, MBG, Jember

articles.headline: Support economic growth, BPOM accelerates investment services for drugs & food
articles.summary: BPOM head Taruna Ikrar said in Jakarta that BPOM will accelerate product registration evaluation, digitise licensing, and simplify regulations to encourage investment in the drug, food, cosmetics sectors and MSMEs. The move aligns with President Prabowo’s directive for an effective bureaucracy, job creation and enhanced industry competitiveness.
articles.keywords: BPOM, Taruna Ikrar, drug regulation, pharmaceutical investment, service digitisation

articles.headline: Supporting economic growth, BPOM accelerates investment services for drugs & food
articles.summary: BPOM head Taruna Ikrar in Jakarta said the agency will speed up product registration evaluation, digitise licensing, and support MSMEs in the drugs, food, cosmetics, and traditional medicine sectors. The move aligns with President Prabowo’s directive for an effective bureaucracy and to boost investment and jobs.

articles.headline: Balai POM Yogyakarta Says Illegal Drug Circulation Remains High, Triggering Youth Crime Actions
articles.summary: Balai Besar POM Yogyakarta secured 157,197 pieces of OOT worth Rp207,285,200 and reported a 19‑fold increase in high‑risk areas over seven years. On 21 May 2026 they held a National Action to Prevent OOT Misuse, emphasizing health and crime threats to adolescents.

articles.headline: BPOM Reaffirms Mandatory Pharmacist in Minimarkets Is a Hoax
articles.summary: BPOM head Taruna Ikrar clarified that PerBPOM No 5 of 2026 does not require a pharmacist in minimarkets, but regulates oversight of over‑the‑counter and limited‑sale medicines. He made the statement in Jakarta, rejecting the circulating hoax.
articles.keywords: BPOM, Taruna Ikrar, PerBPOM 5/2026, pharmacist, minimarket

articles.headline: Introducing PRIMA Service, BPOM Ensures Drug Registration in Indonesia Becomes More Transparent and Inclusive
articles.summary: BPOM held a Public Consultation Forum on 18 May 2026 at the Bhinneka Tunggal Ika Hall, Jakarta, introducing the PRIMA service for more transparent and inclusive drug registration. Director Tri Asti Isnariani reported a public satisfaction index of 93.88%.

articles.headline: City reshaping its breath
articles.summary: Surabaya has become a national pilot candidate for Smoke-Free Areas, assessed by the Ministry of Home Affairs and Kementerian Kesehatan. The city implements Perda No 2/2019, which bans smoking in public facilities, achieving high compliance but still facing challenges from vaping.

articles.headline: BPOM uncovers illegal drug syndicate in Bali, 173,000 illegal tablets seized, impacting mental health
articles.summary: BPOM together with police in Denpasar seized 173,000 illegal tablets worth Rp200 million and charged 15 suspects in the 2023‑May 2026 operation. Enforcement Deputy Tubagus Ade Hidayat stressed the danger of OOT for mental health and the effort to eradicate unofficial distribution chains.
articles.keywords: BPOM, illegal drug syndicate, 173000 tablets, Denpasar, Tubagus Ade Hidayat

articles.headline: BPOM Denpasar seizes misused drug disguised as livestock vitamin
articles.summary: BPOM Denpasar seized 173,000 tablets of illegal drug disguised as livestock vitamin, as part of the National Action to Prevent Misuse of Certain Drugs. The seizure occurred on Wednesday, May 20 in Denpasar, Bali.

articles.headline: BPOM finds over 1 billion illegal specific medicines in West Java and Central Java
articles.summary: BPOM discovered more than one billion tablets of certain medicines (OOT) worth Rp398 billion at illegal factories in West Java and Central Java, mainly in Bogor and Depok. BPOM chief Taruna Ikrar said OOT misuse has risen 19‑fold over seven years, threatening youth. The crackdown involved 46 operations, education for 115 schools and plans to intervene in 1,369 schools in Bogor Regency.

articles.headline: BPOM strengthens oversight of drug sales in retail through PerBPOM 5/2026
articles.summary: BPOM head Taruna Ikrar announced BPOM Regulation No 5/2026, expanding oversight of drug sales to hypermarkets, supermarkets and minimarkets in Jakarta. The rule closes the grey zone by assigning management responsibility and administrative penalties for violations, following PP 28/2024 on the Health Law.
articles.keywords: BPOM, PerBPOM 5/2026, drug supervision, hypermarket, pharmaceutical regulation

articles.headline: BPOM strengthens oversight of drug sales in retail through PerBPOM 5/2026
Without Digital Transformation, Menkes Says It Is Impossible to Deliver Quality Health Services
articles.summary: TRIBUNNEWS.COM – Digital transformation in health is seen as essential to strengthen the national health system and ensure service equity across Indonesia. The point was made by Minister of Health (Menkes) Budi Gunadi Sadikin to the House of Representatives’ Commission IX.

articles.headline: Pariaman seeks to upgrade RSUD type to strengthen tsunami disaster mitigation
articles.summary: Pariaman City Government requested that RSUD dr. Sadikin be upgraded from type D to C in a meeting with Kemenkes, Deputy Minister Benjamin Paulus Octavianus, and DPR member Ade Rezki Pratama on May 19. The upgrade is expected to enhance health services and tsunami mitigation, while the mitigation blueprint will be submitted to Bappenas and BNPB.
articles.keywords: RSUD dr. Sadikin, type C, tsunami, Pariaman, Kementerian Kesehatan

articles.headline: BPOM Uncovers Illegal Drug Syndicate in Bali, Seizes 173,000 Tablets: If You Haven’t Used It, Don’t Use It
articles.summary: BPOM and the police in Denpasar seized 173,000 illegal drug tablets worth Rp200 million and charged 15 suspects from 2023 to May 2026. Enforcement Deputy Tubagus Ade Hidayat said the operation targets the OOT distribution network and protects public health.

articles.headline: AstraZeneca and Roche Diagnostics Asia Pacific Strengthen AI‑Based Digital Pathology Development for Breast and Lung Cancer
articles.summary: AstraZeneca and Roche Diagnostics Asia Pacific have signed a three‑year MoU to develop AI‑based digital pathology for breast and lung cancer across nine Asian countries. The program includes education, training and enhanced testing of HER2 and TROP2 biomarkers, aiming to close diagnostic gaps in the region.

articles.headline: Gathering Public Aspirations from Four Regencies, Balai POM Tabalong Holds Public Consultation Forum
articles.summary: Balai POM Tabalong organized a Public Consultation Forum on 20 May 2026 at Balai Rakyat Dandung Suchrowardi, involving four regencies—Tabalong, Balangan, Hulu Sungai Utara, and Hulu Sungai Tengah. The event aimed to gather community aspirations and strengthen coordination between the government and stakeholders in drug and food oversight.

articles.headline: BBPOM Samarinda holds education to prevent misuse of certain medicines
articles.summary: BBPOM Samarinda organized a prevention education on the misuse of certain medicines (OOT) in the KPPN Samarinda Hall, led by BBPOM Head Agung Kurniawan. The event involved the Director of Narcotics Research of Polda Kaltim and the Narcotics Rehabilitation Center, emphasizing cross‑sector collaboration to protect young people.
articles.keywords: BBPOM, Samarinda, OOT, education, drug misuse

articles.headline: West Papua provincial government integrates mobile health services in Manokwari
articles.summary: The West Papua Health Office launched an integration program of family herbal medicine and acupressure with mobile health services in Manokwari, held from 15–20 May 2026 in the villages of Syou, Amber, and Aserbey. The program involved pediatric, ENT, and other specialists, and follows Permenkes No 9/2016 on self‑care.
articles.keywords: Manokwari, West Papua, mobile health services, family herbal medicine, acupressure

articles.headline: Government Asked to Restructure Process for Drafting Nicotine‑Tar Limit Regulations
articles.summary: Economist Achmad Nur Hidayat (UPN Veteran Jakarta) says the nicotine‑tar limit discussion should involve the Ministry of Agriculture and the Ministry of Manpower, but the Kemenko PMK team excluded them. He warns that regulation lacking social‑economic considerations could harm millions of farmers and tobacco workers.

articles.headline: BPOM: Integration of Safe Food District/City strengthens citizen protection
articles.summary: BPOM’s Deputy for Processed Food Surveillance, Elin Herlina, explained that the Safe Food District/City program (KKPA) will integrate monitoring across the entire food chain to support food safety and inflation control. The target is to reach 28% of districts/cities by 2026, with Semarang winning the 2025 national award with a score of 96.31.

articles.headline: BPOM Payakumbuh strengthens oversight and services through public consultation forum
articles.summary: BPOM Payakumbuh held a Public Consultation Forum on May 19 in the Bappeda Litbang Hall of Tanah Datar Regency, involving local government, business actors, schools and universities in the area. BPOM head Dio Ramondrana said the input will be used to evaluate services and highlighted cooperation with BGN as well as routine inspections of medicines and food.
articles.keywords: BPOM Payakumbuh,Public Consultation Forum,Tanah Datar,Dio Ramondrana,BGN

articles.headline: Pharmacists Not Playing Around
articles.summary: BPOM issued Regulation No 5/2026 expanding the definition of drug‑handling facilities to include hypermarkets, supermarkets and minimarkets. Sales may only be conducted by certified health‑support personnel under pharmacist supervision, limited to over‑the‑counter medicines with a three‑day supply. The policy has sparked strong protests from pharmacists.

articles.headline: Spotlight on BPOM Regulation on Drug Sale Supervision, PC IAI Kotabaru Chair Worried About Dextro Circulation
articles.summary: BPOM issued Regulation No. 5/2026 on supervision of drug sales in pharmaceutical facilities, including retail, effective 6 April 2026. PC IAI Kotabaru chair, Apt Lisa Gunawan, warned that unmonitored circulation of Dextro and pseudoephedrine could be misused, and emphasized the pharmacist’s role in drug management.

articles.headline: BPOM Loosens Drug Distribution. For the Red and White Cooperative?
articles.summary: BPOM issued a regulation allowing modern retail facilities to manage and dispense both over‑the‑counter and limited‑sale drugs. The policy has sparked debate among health workers over drug safety and accessibility.
articles.keywords: BPOM, drug distribution, modern retail, over-the-counter drugs, health workers

articles.headline: Pharmacy for Village Cooperatives
articles.summary: The government, through Presidential Instruction No. 9 of 2025, permits village cooperatives to operate clinics and pharmacies and shifts drug oversight to a distribution system involving non‑pharmacist personnel. The new BPOM regulation raises concerns about patient safety and puts pressure on small pharmacies.

articles.headline: Modern retail in Banjarmasin limits medicine sales, only sells green‑logo products
articles.summary: BPOM issued Regulation No. 5 of 2026 requiring a special permit to sell blue‑ or red‑logo medicines. As a result, minimarkets in Banjarmasin only sell green‑logo products such as telon oil and Antangin, while pharmacists criticize the policy.

articles.headline: BPOM Regulation Makes Pharmacists in South Kalimantan Uneasy, Supermarket Staff to Be Trained in Drug Management
articles.summary: The BPOM regulation makes pharmacists in South Kalimantan uneasy, and supermarket staff will be trained to manage medicines. They consider the brief training for retail support staff insufficient to deeply understand drug use.

articles.headline: Don't Be Lured by Low Prices, Check the Signs and List of Dangerous Cosmetics
articles.summary: The surge of cosmetics on online marketplaces means consumers must be more vigilant when choosing beauty products. Low‑price offers can be deceptive, and many products may contain harmful ingredients.

articles.headline: Hard Drugs Found in Supermarket, See BPOM Rules and Response
articles.summary: A netizen discovered hard drugs being sold in a supermarket. BPOM has outlined the applicable regulations and issued its response.
